extraction.one_line_summary: BERNAL Egan attacked the final gravel climb at Campo Felice, overtaking the breakaway at top speed to take his first Grand Tour stage win.
tactical_patterns: BERNAL attacked in big ring on gravel climb, sprinting through breakaway riders rather than tempo increase—extreme power display.
INEOS used MOSCON domestique pace on final climb to launch BERNAL, classic team tempo-to-attack sequence controlling GC group.
BOUCHARD built virtual mountains lead over penultimate climb before stage attack, suggesting jersey calculus influenced break composition timing.
Break took 70km to form with GC riders involved, revealing teams uncertain whether to control or gamble early.
VLASOV attacked before BERNAL moved, forcing pink jersey's hand rather than waiting—Astana probing INEOS strength.

rider_signals.BERNAL Egan: positive: sprinted big ring on 14% gravel finish overtaking breakaway, exceptional climbing power suggests peak form return
rider_signals.CICCONE Giulio: positive: only rider briefly holding BERNAL's initial acceleration, marked resurgence on hard gravel gradient
rider_signals.VLASOV Aleksandr: positive: third on stage attacking before BERNAL, showing willingness to race aggressively despite GC deficit
rider_signals.BOUCHARD Geoffrey: positive: went solo from break on penultimate climb targeting mountains jersey, strong enough nearly to hold stage
rider_signals.VALTER Attila: negative: lost 49 seconds and pink jersey on gravel climb, lacks explosive power on steep irregular gradients
rider_signals.MOSCON Gianni: positive: paced final climb for BERNAL showing INEOS depth, critical domestique work setting decisive tempo
rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: neutral: fourth at 7 seconds, limited time loss but unable to follow BERNAL's acceleration
rider_signals.MARTIN Dan: neutral: fifth at 7 seconds, solid GC defense but no punch to contest stage
rider_signals.CARUSO Damiano: positive: tenth on stage in GC group despite earlier being in break, exceptional recovery suggests deep fitness
rider_signals.CARR Simon: neutral: attacked first from break but caught, classic move timing without legs to finish
